protocol_definition.name: Single Agent
protocol_definition.message_flow: One agent receives the task and produces the final output.
protocol_definition.visibility: The agent sees only the configured task view.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after the final output or when the interaction budget is reached.

messages.content: You are a careful single-agent baseline. Complete the task directly using the required format and visible task information.

Complete this benchmark task as a single-agent baseline.

Task ID: SE-04

Category: Software Engineering

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After a file is uploaded, the system should:

1. Validate the file type and size.
2. Store the original file and its metadata.
3. Extract the document text.
4. Divide the text into searchable sections.
5. Create a searchable index.
6. Allow users to search the processed documents.
7. Report the processing status of each uploaded file.

The design must also support the following requirements:

- Files may be processed asynchronously.
- A failed processing step should be retryable.
- Repeated uploads of the same file should not create unnecessary duplicate records.
- New document formats should be easy to add later.
- The design should remain understandable and maintainable for a small development team.

Produce a software design that explains the system components, data flow, API interface, data model, code organization, error-handling strategy, and testing plan.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, or code examples may be included where they help clarify the design.

Required Output Format:
Use the following structure:

1. System overview
2. Main components and responsibilities
3. End-to-end processing workflow
4. REST API design
5. Data model
6. Suggested project structure
7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention
8. Testing plan
9. Design tradeoff

Include the following two tables.

### Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|

### API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|

The response should be approximately 700–900 words.

Follow the required output format exactly. Do not use external tools if the task prohibits them.

final_output:
1. System overview
The service is a backend for a document-processing and search platform. Users upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. Each upload undergoes validation, storage, text extraction, segmentation into searchable sections, and indexing. Users can perform searches against processed documents and monitor per-file processing status. The system supports asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, deduplication of repeated uploads, and easy extension to new formats. The design emphasizes maintainability for a small team with clear module boundaries and simple data flows.

2. Main components and responsibilities

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / HTTP interface | Accepts uploads, searches, and status queries; authenticates callers | HTTP requests (multipart upload, search query) | HTTP responses with status, results |
| Upload Service | Validates file type/size, deduplicates, stores original + metadata | File + metadata (user, filename, size, hash) |Acknowledge receipt; job id; storage references |
| Deduplication & Metadata Repository | Detects duplicate uploads; stores file metadata | File hash, metadata | Existing/new file record reference |
| Storage Layer | Persistent store for original files and metadata | Original binary files, metadata | File storage location pointer, metadata record |
| Processing Orchestrator | Coordinates async processing; queues tasks; retries failed steps | Job/Task requests from Upload Service | Processing job status updates, next-step triggers |
| Text Extraction Module | Extracts text from documents (PDF, TXT, Markdown) | Original file | Raw text, per-file metadata |
| Document Segmentation Module | Splits text into searchable sections (paragraphs, sections) | Raw text | Text sections with positions/ids |
| Indexing Service | Builds and maintains search index over sections | Text sections + metadata | Search index entries (inverted index) |
| Search Service | Executes user queries against index; returns relevant documents/sections | Query text | Ranked results with snippets and document references |
| Status & Monitoring Service | Tracks processing status per file; exposes status API | Processing events | Status records (queued, processing, done, failed) |
| Configuration & Extensibility Layer | Centralizes formats, validators, and pipelines | Format definitions | Configured pipelines; new format hooks |

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- Upload
  - User uploads a PDF, TXT, or Markdown file with metadata (user id, filename, size).
  - API validates file type/size; computes a content hash for deduplication.
  - If identical file hash exists, return existing document reference (no duplicate records).
  - Otherwise, store the original file and metadata; create a processing job and enqueue to Processing Orchestrator.

- Processing pipeline (asynchronous)
  - Processing Orchestrator picks up the job; marks step as in-progress.
  - Text Extraction Module extracts text from the original file.
  - If extraction fails, mark step failed; provide retry logic (e.g., exponential backoff); on max retries, mark file as failed.
  - Segmenter splits extracted text into sections with identifiers and positions.
  - Indexer ingests sections into a searchable inverted index, indexing fields such as document id, section id, text content, and metadata.
  - Status Service records progression: queued -> in-progress -> completed or failed.
  - On completion, update the document record with processing status; expose searchable index availability.

- Search
  - User submits a search query via REST API.
  - Search Service queries the inverted index; returns ranked results with document references and snippet highlights.
  - If the document is not yet fully processed, the API can indicate pending status or omit results until ready.

- Status reporting
  - Clients query per-file status; the Status Service exposes current state and last update timestamp.

4. REST API design

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /uploads | Upload a file for processing | Multipart: file, user_id, filename, size | { job_id, document_id, status: "queued" } |
| GET | /uploads/{document_id}/status | Retrieve processing status | - | { document_id, status, steps: { extraction, segmentation, indexing }, last_updated } |
| GET | /search | Search across processed documents | query, user_id(optional), limit, offset | { results: [{ document_id, score, snippet, metadata }], total } |
| GET | /documents/{document_id} | Retrieve document metadata and processing state | - | { document_id, status, metadata, created_at, processed_at, index_ready: boolean } |

Notes:
- Authentication/authorization can be layered (API keys, OAuth) but kept simple for the baseline.
- Search endpoint returns only documents with completed processing unless the client opts to see in-progress results.

5. Data model

- Document
  - document_id: UUID
  - user_id: string
  - filename: string
  - size_bytes: int
  - hash: string (content hash for dedup)
  - status: enum { queued, extracting, segmented, indexed, ready, failed }
  - created_at: timestamp
  - updated_at: timestamp
  - index_ready: boolean (derived from status)

- ProcessingStep
  - step_name: enum { extraction, segmentation, indexing }
  - status: enum { pending, in_progress, done, failed }
  - last_updated: timestamp
  - retries: int
  - error_message: string (optional)

- OriginalFileStorage
  - storage_uri: string (location of the stored file)
  - mime_type: string
  - encoding: string (optional)

- TextContent
  - document_id: FK -> Document
  - full_text: text
  - extracted_at: timestamp

- Section
  - section_id: UUID
  - document_id: FK -> Document
  - start_offset: int
  - end_offset: int
  - text: text
  - metadata: JSON (e.g., heading, paragraph order)

- IndexEntry
  - term: string
  - document_id: FK -> Document
  - section_id: FK -> Section
  - weight: float
  - metadata: JSON

- SearchResult
  - document_id
  - snippet: string
  - score: float
  - metadata: JSON

6. Suggested project structure

- api/
  - routes/
  - handlers/
- services/
  - upload/
  - dedupe/
  - processing/
  - text_extraction/
  - segmentation/
  - indexing/
  - search/
  - status/
- model/
  - document.py
  - section.py
  - index_entry.py
- storage/
  - blob_store.py
  - metadata_store.py
- config/
  - config.py
- tests/
  - unit/
  - integration/
- workers/ (if using a worker pool)
  - worker.py
- docs/
  - design.md

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Validation errors (unsupported type, size too large) return 4xx with actionable message; do not create a processing job.
- Deduplication:
  - Compute stable content_hash (e.g., SHA-256) from the file content.
  - If a document with the same hash exists, return existing document_id and do not create a new record or duplicate storage.
- Retry policy:
  - Processing steps have a per-step retry counter with exponential backoff (e.g., start at 1s, cap at 5 minutes).
  - Temporarily failing steps (e.g., transient I/O) are retried automatically.
  - After max_retries, mark document as failed and notify via status endpoint; allow manual retry trigger.
- Idempotent uploads:
  - If a client retries the same upload (same hash), the system deduplicates and returns the same document_id without side effects.
- Observability:
  - Centralized logging for each step; structured logs with document_id and step.
  - Metrics for queue depth, processing times, retry counts.
- Extensibility:
  - Add new formats by introducing a new extractor plugin with a standard interface; register in Configuration Layer.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests
  - Validate file type/size validators; hash computation.
  - Text extraction stubs for PDF/TXT/Markdown; ensure correct call paths.
  - Segmenter correctness on representative samples.
  - Indexing correctness: small inverted index correctness checks.
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end upload flow with a mock storage backend; verify deduplication behavior.
  - Asynchronous processing pipeline: verify correct step progression and status updates.
  - Retry path: force a failure in a step and confirm exponential backoff and eventual success or final failure.
- API tests
  - Upload with valid/invalid data; status retrieval; search results after indexing.
- Performance/scaling tests
  - Large documents and multiple concurrent uploads; observe queueing and indexing latency.
- Regression tests
  - Ensure adding a new document format does not break existing formats.

9. Design tradeoff

- Simplicity vs. scalability: The design favors readability and maintainability for a small team over ultra-high scalability. Asynchronous processing with a straightforward queue and explicit per-step status minimizes complexity.
- Deduplication via content hash reduces storage and duplicates but requires reading entire file content to compute hash; acceptable for moderate-file sizes and ensures idempotent uploads.
- Pluggable format modules allow easy extension to new formats; however, it adds initial coupling between extractor interfaces and pipeline steps. This is mitigated by a clear plugin contract and a registry.
- Search index design uses an inverted index per document with sections; this balances recall and snippet generation. For very large datasets, a separate search engine (e.g., specialized service) could be introduced later without changing core workflow.

Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / HTTP interface | Accepts uploads, searches, and status queries; authenticates callers | HTTP requests | HTTP responses |
| Upload Service | Validates file type/size, deduplicates, stores original + metadata | File + metadata | Acknowledge receipt; job id; storage references |
| Deduplication & Metadata Repository | Detects duplicate uploads; stores file metadata | File hash, metadata | Existing/new file record reference |
| Storage Layer | Persistent store for original files and metadata | Original files, metadata | Storage pointers, metadata records |
| Processing Orchestrator | Coordinates async processing; queues tasks; retries steps | Job requests | Processing events, next-step triggers |
| Text Extraction Module | Extracts text from documents | Original file | Raw text, metadata |
| Document Segmentation Module | Splits text into searchable sections | Raw text | Text sections with ids |
| Indexing Service | Builds/maintains search index | Text sections + metadata | Index entries |
| Search Service | Executes queries against index | Query text | Ranked results |
| Status & Monitoring Service | Tracks per-file processing status | Processing events | Status records |
| Configuration & Extensibility Layer | Centralizes formats/validators | Config data | Configured pipelines |

API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /uploads | Upload a file for processing | Multipart: file, user_id, filename, size | { job_id, document_id, status: "queued" } |
| GET | /uploads/{document_id}/status | Retrieve processing status | - | { document_id, status, steps, last_updated } |
| GET | /search | Search across processed documents | query, user_id, limit, offset | { results: [{ document_id, score, snippet, metadata }], total } |
| GET | /documents/{document_id} | Retrieve document metadata | - | { document_id, status, metadata, created_at, processed_at, index_ready } |

This design provides a clear, maintainable blueprint for building a document-processing and search backend with asynchronous processing, robust error handling, and straightforward extension points for new formats and features.

agent_effective_config.description: Low-latency OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.
